3106.name: The Gardens
3106.description: A tiny pond teeming with life lies next to you, giving rise to thousands of mushrooms growing thirstily at its side. An oortaag pauses to take a long drink from the pool, and then brushes past your leg amiably as it moves westward, toward Ramluf Plaza.

3108.name: The Gilded Boar
3108.description: A smoky haze fills this spacious hollow, filling your nose with a tingling sensation that makes you feel perpetually on the verge of sneezing. Several fireplaces filled with burning logs ward off the chill of the caverns, surrounded by the drunken dwarves which lay sprawled about the establishment. Busty tavern wenches amble about, their eyes closing with exhaustion as they scrounge for a few obsidian.

npcs.name: Tipsy veteran
npcs.description: Buy him another round and he's your best friend until he passes out

3109.name: Jova Street
3109.description: A broad avenue runs perpendicular to the Walk of Vanari, passing numerous dwellings carved into the rock. The glow of burning fires fills most of the curtained windows along the street, giving the area a pleasant feel despite the perpetual twilight of the underground city.

3113.name: Jova Street
3113.description: You trudge quietly along the lengthy street, a bit perturbed by the odd silence that hangs over this part of the city. A decidedly seedy establishment reeking of ale warns you away to the south, and Jova Street continues to the east and west.

3114.name: Banyor's Way
3114.description: You walk along a narrow street, choking slightly at the musty fragrance of the underground gardens that flourish to the northeast. The sounds of quiet conversation and an occasional peal of silvery laughter drift in from your surroundings, offering snatches of the simple lives of the humble Vanari.

299.name: Abandoned Mining Tunnels
299.description: The sounds of mining echo from the north, a patternless repetition of steel striking stone. Fresh air also drifts in from that direction, indicating the tunnel's end. You are riding atop a well-bred horse.

300.name: Abandoned Mining Tunnels
300.description: Forgotten tunnels surround you, nearly all of which have been allowed to cave in after their yields petered out. The main thoroughfare continues to the west and east. You are riding atop a well-bred horse.

npcs.name: Old miner
npcs.description: He's seen a lot more than you have, youngster.

301.name: Abandoned Mining Tunnels
301.description: The walls of the tunnel are cracked and broken from persistent hammering in search of mineral deposits. Numerous similar tunnels branch off from here, but almost all have been boarded shut. You are riding atop a well-bred horse.

298.name: Dwarven Excavation
298.description: A choking cloud of dust hangs heavily in the air, sending your nose to immediate twitching. Dwarven miners are clustered at the walls of the pit, hammering mechanically at the stone. A crude wooden ladder leans precariously against the east face, offering escape from the dust. You are riding atop a well-bred horse.

npcs.name: Burly dwarf
npcs.description: Looks like hot work.

303.name: Ramluf Plaza
303.description: Maybe it wasn't such a good idea to enter the merchant quarter. If you remain here much longer some sharp-minded businessman is sure to sucker you into a purchase that you're not ready for. Wringing your hands anxiously, you cast about in search of a way out. You are riding atop a well-bred horse.

306.name: North Garden Entrance
306.description: A series of gaudily colored fungi sprout all about your feet, scrambling for the height needed for proper spore distribution. Tiny insects flit about, finding food among the dense fungal bloom. Ramluf Plaza teems with equal activity to the north.

npcs.name: Curious holiim
npcs.description: eyes that constitute most of its face.  Apparently no one told it how

npcs.name: Yowling laarnog
npcs.description: You have repeatedly thought that the caverns never fail to produce odd beasts, and the little laarnog standing before you seems to confirm your beliefs.  You really don't want to know how such a thing was created.

307.name: Centre of the Gardens
307.description: Lolara plants grow all about you, reaching far above the ground to brush at the cavern ceiling high above. How anything can grow to such astounding heights in this underground atmosphere is incomprehensible. Indistinct paths of trampled moss lead off in all directions, wandering about to other areas of the gardens.

308.name: East Garden Entrance
308.description: Tiny creepers work their way about the plant life here, winding around the local flora with surprising tenacity. A mavloor stumbles past, screeching insanely as it trips along. Muradian Avenue lies to the east, marking an abrupt end to the lush vegetation.

309.name: Muradian Avenue
309.description: The fanciful stonework surrounding you speaks of great wealth; there is little doubt in your mind as to which of the three classes holds most of the money. A part of you wonders how this came to be, but you decide that it would probably be best to avoid involving yourself in the hectic politics of the dwarves.

npcs.name: Curious holiim
npcs.description: eyes that constitute most of its face.  Apparently no one told it how

npcs.name: Roaming oortaag
npcs.description: pausing now and then to sniff the air with its snouts.

304.name: Ramluf Plaza
304.description: Officious merchants amble about at a stately pace, obviously far too important to move along at a decent rate. You begin to feel a bit insecure about the size of your purse as you run your eyes over the expensively furnished stores arranged about the plaza, wondering if you'll be able to afford a trinket of some sort to impress the folks back home.

npcs.name: Bumbling mavloor
npcs.description: it's very happy doing just whatever it's doing, so why not leave it be?

305.name: Ramluf Plaza
305.description: Apprentices scramble about you, running from place to place as they attempt to complete the impossible tasks laid upon them by their masters. You stumble over a young fellow who has long since passed out from exhaustion, thoughtfully flip him an obsidian, and set off with your nose twitching in anticipation of striking a bargain in the bustling merchant quarter.

310.name: Realm of Wizardry
310.description: Rows of potions lie in a neatly arranged fashion, accompanied by carefully labelled bags of reagents and other magical oddities. Wands of varying colors and sizes stand side by side in a rack set against the wall, and wax-sealed scrolls occupy a few small barrels tucked into a corner.

312.name: Walk of Vanari
312.description: A lazy air hangs over the Vanari's part of the city, seemingly unmindful of the pressing concerns of the other classes. The sprawling underground gardens that the dwarves have cultivated over the years spread out to your east, and Banyor's Way wanders off to the north and south, skirting the carefully planted vegetation.

315.name: Common Market
315.description: The clamorous cacophony of the crowd assaults your ears as you wander the market, eagerly hunting for a bargain. Brightly colored bolts of cloth and odd little trinkets are stacked everywhere, accompanied by platoons of yowling dwarves hoping to make a sale. Pleased with yourself for avoiding the third- rate shops offering worthless junk, you turn about in search of a quality establishment. A bumbling mavloor stumbles in from the east.

e.to_room_name: Varli's House of Genuine Magical Paraphernalia

npcs.name: Burly guardsman
npcs.description: It would probably be best to avoid arousing his anger.  He's probably ready to burst, judging by the way he grimaces and mutters as he shifts

316.name: Varli's House of Genuine Magical Paraphernalia
316.description: Astounding! Stupendous! You are simply amazed at how this old bugger has managed to stay in business! Untold hundreds of useless, "magical" items festooned with reflecting quartz stones and glittering glass "gems" wink at you from their positions atop the shelves, supervised by the roving eye of old Varli the Magician.

317.name: Vanari Gatehouse
317.description: Monolithic walls rise about you; these, coupled with the heavy portcullis that rests above, would prevent all possible entry into the city should the dwarves decide that visitors are unwelcome. Awed by such solid construction, you stare about in respectful silence.

313.name: Walk of Vanari
313.description: The banter of old warriors fills the air here, setting your ears aflame with their offensive subjects. No, you just won't believe it. There's simply no way one dwarf could have been involved with that many women. Shaking your head at such foolishness, you hurry by the crowd of lounging veterans positioned outside a shabby tavern.

npcs.name: Bumbling mavloor
npcs.description: it's very happy doing just whatever it's doing, so why not leave it be?

311.name: West Garden Entrance
311.description: A lush, mossy vegetation grows beneath your feet, spreading to cover every inch of unplanted ground. The heady aroma of countless fungi assails your nose, making thought difficult. A narrow path offers egress to the west.

318.name: West Gate
318.description: Stocky dwarves push past you as you stand before the gate, straining to enter the bustling city. Thickly muscled watchmen supervise the flow of citizenry, fingering their cudgels and eyeing your movements carefully. Cavern walls worn smooth by the constant press of dwarves prevent travel to the north and south, a well-lit passage runs west, and the city buzzes with activity to the east. The gloves seem to meld into the shadows around you, your movements becoming nothing more than a whisper in the wind. Each step you take is light, effortless, as if the shadows themselves are guiding your path. You relax and return to a more normal gait.

330.name: Submerged Grotto
330.description: The current swirls about you, pulling you toward the south wall as it dashes along. You glance above you and notice a long shaft driving upward, perhaps it leads to the surface?

npcs.name: Playful tuvik
npcs.description: of the tuvik that are not covered with a luxurious fur coat.

npcs.name: Slothful mavjir
npcs.description: suddenly lethargic.

331.name: Submerged Grotto
331.description: The water to the east bears an almost imperceptible tinge of light, although perhaps it is merely your imagination fabricating the image which you are eagerly praying for. The grotto winds west and south into darkness, making the east path a much more attractive option.

332.name: Submerged Grotto
332.description: Silence reigns in this corner of the grotto, encompassing you in a suffocating shroud of absolute stillness. The sound of your passage is muffled by an unseen force, maintaining the solemn aura of the area.

npcs.name: Kyagh
npcs.description: gills that lines his body convinces you that he descended from some long

333.name: Submerged Grotto
333.description: The walls here are scarred and pitted, and chunks of broken rock lie scattered at your feet. A moss-like substance clings tenaciously to the ceiling, trailing long creepers into the sluggish current here in the grotto.

npcs.name: Playful tuvik
npcs.description: of the tuvik that are not covered with a luxurious fur coat.

npcs.name: Xanig the Hammerfisted
npcs.description: impressively muscled arms terminate in blocklike hands that can pulverize

334.name: Submerged Grotto
334.description: The utter blackness that surrounds you deep in the caverns defies description. All sense of motion is lost as you trudge along in the unchanging darkness, hoping to escape this featureless hell.

335.name: Submerged Grotto
335.description: The faint lapping of water against an exposed surface is barely audible here, seemingly originating from somewhere above you. You peer upward, and are surprised to find that the water's limit is no more than a few feet above your head. Kicking off the cavern floor in anticipation of a welcome breath of air, you swim toward the surface.

336.name: Submerged Grotto
336.description: The stark cavern walls seem to press in on you as you stare at the unrelieved expanse of barren stone. An occasional fish swims past, probably as anxious as you to leave these monotonous surroundings.

338.name: Submerged Grotto
338.description: You stumble along the broken floor of the grotto, cursing a long stream of bubbles every time your foot slips on the treacherous, gravelly bits of rock. Preoccupied with inventing new expletives, you finally look up and watch with mounting horror as a fish with gaping jaws emerges from a hole in the cavern wall and swims toward you with terrifying speed.

npcs.name: Spearfish
npcs.description: blinding speed, its powerful tail flicking from side to side as it

339.name: Submerged Grotto
339.description: The water is oddly still here; the familiar tug of the gentle currents is entirely absent. The underwater caverns continue to the north, or you may escape to the lake bottom to the south.

340.name: Before an Opening
340.description: A monolithic mountain of stone has shouldered its way up into the lake to the north, making further travel in that direction impossible unless you wish to risk whatever dangers lurk in the underwater grotto that winds into the rock. A meandering mavjir pauses to study you; its single eye seems to dare you to enter. Thrusting your jaw out pugnaciously at the offensive beast, you move forward to investigate the entrance.

341.name: Lake Bottom
341.description: The current tugs at you quite strongly here, pulling you away from the wall of broken stone rising to the north. Digging your feet into the sandy floor to maintain your position, you stop to consider if it might be possible to somehow proceed past the rock.

npcs.name: Slothful mavjir
npcs.description: suddenly lethargic.

342.name: Lake Bottom
342.description: You stand on the shifting sands that comprise the floor of the lake, watching the activity of the fishes around you in fascination. You notice a thick stalk rising from beneath the sand, and are then transfixed with terror as its bulbous top slides open to reveal a luminous eye staring directly at you. Flight seems prudent right now.

npcs.name: Rahljia
npcs.description: imaginary creatures that parents use to scare their children into

2262.name: Outdoor Outfitters
2262.description: General supplies of all sorts have been hung on pegs driven into the cavern wall. Small traveling packs, waterskins, and lanterns all lie within easy reach, and at reasonable prices. That's quite surprising, considering some of the costs you've seen in other areas of the merchant quarter. You are riding atop a well-bred horse.

346.name: Lake Bottom
346.description: Tall, aquatic plants wave languidly in the gentle currents at the bottom of the lake, swaying in an endless dance. Tiny crustaceans scuttle about the sandy floor, their movements amazingly swift in the cumbersome environment. A tuvik swims past, flapping one fin-like paw at you as it heads toward a cave entrance just barely perceptible to the north.

347.name: Adrift on Lake Cadain
347.description: Phosphorescent fish leap merrily about your boat, slipping in and out of the water with ease. The cavern ceiling is lost from view far above you, and for a moment you feel almost as though you were back outside, away from this subterranean gloom.

348.name: Adrift on Lake Cadain
348.description: Your craft spins about in lazy circles, tracing an errant path across the shifting waters. You wouldn't mind floating about for hours were it not for that fact that the whistling winds threaten to capsize you at any minute.

349.name: Floating Just Offshore
349.description: My but it's cold out here. The wind certainly whips across the water's surface at a good clip. Shivering in your flimsy craft, you wonder whether the tepid waters might offer some relief.

350.name: Drifting About
350.description: The roaming currents of Lake Cadain slip beneath you, swirling about randomly. A few fishermen float past, huddling for warmth in their thick cloaks. You don't see how it's possible for them to catch anything, why would surface?

351.name: Shores of Lake Cadain
351.description: You sit upon a mossy rock, dangling your toes in the silvery water of the lake and drinking in the beauty of your surroundings. The troubles of your journeys slip from your mind as you gaze across the unbroken expanse of rippling waters, dreaming of exploring their depths or floating across their delicate surface. The lonesome cry of a bird on the wing reaches your ears, ghosting in from far above the lake.

1632.name: Muradian Avenue
1632.description: A broad staircase climbs the side of a building, leading up to a small landing just barely visible above. The clang of a hammer striking steel disturbs your contemplation of the stairs; you turn to the north and spy a craftsman working on an adventurer's breastplate to improve the fit.

353.name: Banyor's Way
353.description: A leashed oortaag stumps along determinedly, followed by a little dwarf frantically trying to keep up with his pet. Smiling in spite of yourself, you pause to watch the comical procession until the pair disappear around a corner. Wistfully thinking of the carefree days of youth, you turn your gaze to the silvery lake south of the city.

354.name: Banyor's Way
354.description: Several dwarves wander past you, strolling along in pairs and enjoying the quiet beauty of the gardens visible to the northwest. The breathtaking sight of Lake Cadain captures your eye to the south, and the dim cavern-city spreads on all other sides.

355.name: Banyor's Way
355.description: The stylish Muradian section of the city offers amazing sights to the northeast, pleasing the eye with its graceful statues and tasteful houses speckled with precious stones. You are again struck by the odd nature of the dwellings carved deep below the ground, but cannot help admiring the craftsmanship of the dwarven stonemasons.

1633.name: East Gate
1633.description: A heavy gate which appears to be crafted from solid gold has been thrown open to allow the few dwarves entering and leaving the city to pass. You stare at the gates for a long moment, and reluctantly come to the conclusion that they're far too heavy to steal.

352.name: Path to Lake Cadain
352.description: The fungal vegetation of the caves springs up all about you, covering local rocks with a spongy coating that would doubtless be exceedingly comfortable to sleep upon. A profound stillness hangs in the air, as if nature had recognized this place as being far too holy to profane with the sound of worldly creatures. The lake beckons to the south.

2263.name: Vanari Courthouse
2263.description: Polished benches line the floor of this neat little building, facing a tall podium from which a fat little dwarf wearing a ridiculous wig passes judgement on the petty criminals sprawled about the room. He doesn't appear to be going over their crimes very carefully. In fact, he seems to be more interested in the keg of ale standing conveniently at his side. An occasional hiccup reaches your ears.

3107.name: The Shining Stone
3107.description: Wooden tables covered with fine cloth dominate the room, upon most of which the jewels that Nolfar currently has the pleasure of inspecting have been laid out. Occasionally he has been known to make a purchase when a gem strikes his fancy; maybe he might be interested in something you have.

3115.name: Boatyard
3115.description: Shirtless dwarves laboriously haul heavy saws back and forth, cutting beams for construction. Unfinished skeletons lie amid the broken remains of ancient craft. Sweeping your eyes over the assorted woodwork, you look for something usable.

npcs.name: Sawyer
npcs.description: Aren't you glad you chose the life of an adventurer?  It certainly

345.name: Swimming in Lake Cadain
345.description: It's rather peaceful here; there's nothing to disturb you except for the occasional inquisitive tuvik. You giggle foolishly as one of the furry little rascals brushes your leg as it passes, and then, with cheeks flaming in mortification, you glance around to see if anyone noticed your childish outburst.

337.name: An Unexpected Discovery
337.description: You shiver in the chill air, perplexed by the existence of this small cave which the water failed to claim. Your teeth chatter violently as you rise from the gloomy depths of the grotto, hoping to find some material that might fuel a small fire to warm your frigid bones.

npcs.name: Hideous muck leech
npcs.description: wriggles towards you with mindless determination.

344.name: In the Waters of Lake Cadain
344.description: You drift through the silent waters of Cadain, floating effortlessly in your serene surroundings. You pitch forward with a gentle stroke and stare in fascination at the liquid depths beneath you.

343.name: Swimming in Lake Cadain
343.description: Schools of fish swim solemnly about in the cool waters of the lake, seemingly content to spend their lives wandering aimlessly. What a terrible thing it must be to do nothing but go from place to place and attack other creatures.

n.to_room_name: In the Waters of Lake Cadain

1634.name: The Dwelling of Dolmir
1634.description: You follow the stairway to a small landing, and then duck through a low doorway into a pavilion set up above the Artificer's workshop. Thick rugs have been strewn all over the floor for comfort, and iron braziers filled with burning coals add both warmth and a considerable amount of smoke to the little home.
